Details: Data from one pilot-scale study showed greater than 99 percent removal of methomyl two-stage direct filtration. Study water was first circulated 10-20 times through a sand filter that included multiple layers of media, including, fine and medium sand, fine and medium graded gravel, and coarse gravel.  Average removal efficiency through the sand filters was 61 percent.  After being filtered 10-20 times through the sand filter, the water was then filtered once through a carbon filter, which was identical in configuration with the exception that the fine sand layer was replaced by anthracite filter media.  Total removal by the two-stage system averaged greater than 99 percent.  As methomyl is strongly bound to particles, removal may have been due to microorganisms in the sea water matrix.
